name: Blood
description: Your blood does not have enough iron to carry oxygen to all parts of your body.

steps.question: Rohan feels very tired and his skin looks pale. Which nutrient is he missing?
options: Iron
Vitamin C
Calcium
Sugar
steps.explanation: Iron is needed to make hemoglobin in red blood cells. Without iron, blood cannot carry enough oxygen, making you feel tired and pale.

steps.question: Which food will give Rohan the iron he needs to feel better?
steps.explanation: Spinach is one of the best plant sources of iron. Eating it regularly helps your blood carry oxygen properly.

steps.question: Why does spinach help someone with anemia?
options: It is rich in iron which helps make red blood cells
It has lots of sugar for energy
It makes your hair grow faster
It has Vitamin D for strong bones
steps.explanation: Spinach contains iron, which is used by your body to make hemoglobin — the part of red blood cells that carries oxygen.

steps.question: How can you prevent anemia?
options: Eat iron-rich foods like spinach, lentils, and beans every day
Sleep more than 12 hours a day
Drink more soda and sweet juices
Avoid eating green vegetables
steps.explanation: Eating iron-rich foods daily keeps your blood healthy. Lentils, beans, leafy greens, and fortified cereals are all great sources of iron.

steps.question: What is the job of hemoglobin in the blood?
options: It carries oxygen from the lungs to all body parts
It digests the food we eat
It helps us see in the dark
It makes our bones strong
steps.explanation: Hemoglobin is a special protein found in red blood cells. It picks up oxygen in the lungs and delivers it to every part of the body.

name: Eyes
description: Without enough Vitamin A, your eyes cannot adjust to see in dim or dark places.

steps.question: Rohan cannot see well when the lights are dim. Which vitamin is he missing?
options: Vitamin A
Vitamin B
Vitamin K
Vitamin E
steps.explanation: Vitamin A is needed to make a substance called rhodopsin in the eyes, which helps us see in low light.

steps.question: Which food will help Rohan's eyes see better in the dark?
steps.explanation: Carrots are packed with beta-carotene, which your body turns into Vitamin A. Vitamin A keeps your eyes healthy and helps you see in low light.

steps.question: Why do carrots help with night blindness?
options: They contain beta-carotene which becomes Vitamin A in the body
They make your eyes turn orange
They have calcium for strong teeth
They taste sweet so they give energy
steps.explanation: Carrots contain beta-carotene, a pigment that the body converts into Vitamin A. Vitamin A is essential for making rhodopsin, the eye pigment needed for night vision.

steps.question: How can you prevent night blindness?
options: Eat Vitamin A rich foods like carrots, sweet potato, and leafy greens
Wear sunglasses at all times
Avoid looking at lights
Eat more sweets and chocolates
steps.explanation: Eating Vitamin A rich foods every day keeps your eyes healthy. Good sources include carrots, sweet potato, mango, and dark leafy vegetables.

steps.question: Which part of the eye is responsible for seeing in dim light?
options: Rod cells in the retina
Cone cells in the cornea
The lens of the eye
The iris of the eye
steps.explanation: Rod cells are special light-sensitive cells in the retina. They need Vitamin A to work properly and help us see in low light conditions.

name: Bones
description: Without enough Vitamin D, bones become soft and weak, causing pain and deformity in children.

steps.question: Rohan's legs hurt when he walks and his bones feel soft. Which vitamin is he missing?
options: Vitamin D
Vitamin C
Vitamin B12
Vitamin A
steps.explanation: Vitamin D helps the body absorb calcium from food. Without Vitamin D, bones do not get enough calcium and become soft — this is called Rickets.

steps.question: Which food will give Rohan Vitamin D to strengthen his bones?
steps.explanation: Milk is fortified with Vitamin D and also contains calcium. Together they make bones strong and healthy.

steps.question: Why does drinking milk help prevent Rickets?
options: Milk has Vitamin D and calcium which work together to build strong bones
Milk makes you taller faster
Milk contains iron for healthy blood
Milk has sugar that gives you energy
steps.explanation: Milk provides both Vitamin D and calcium. Vitamin D helps the intestines absorb calcium, and calcium is the main mineral that makes bones hard and strong.

steps.question: What is the best way to prevent Rickets in children?
options: Drink milk, eat fish, and spend some time in sunlight every day
Eat lots of sugar and sweets
Stay indoors and rest all day
Drink cold drinks instead of milk
steps.explanation: Sunlight helps your skin make Vitamin D naturally. Eating dairy, fish, and eggs also provides Vitamin D. All of these together keep bones strong.

steps.question: Which mineral makes up most of the hard structure of bones?
options: Calcium
Iron
Sodium
Potassium
steps.explanation: Calcium is the most important mineral in bones. It is stored in bones and gives them their hardness and strength. Vitamin D helps the body absorb calcium from food.

name: Lungs
description: Low Vitamin C weakens the lungs and makes it harder to breathe during exercise or illness.

steps.question: Rohan gets out of breath easily and catches colds very often. Which vitamin is he missing?
options: Vitamin C
Vitamin D
Vitamin K
Vitamin B1
steps.explanation: Vitamin C is a powerful antioxidant that protects the lungs, strengthens the immune system, and helps the body fight infections.

steps.question: Which food will give Rohan Vitamin C to help his lungs and immune system?
steps.explanation: Oranges are one of the richest sources of Vitamin C. Eating one orange a day can boost your immune system and protect your lungs.

steps.question: Why do oranges help keep your lungs healthy?
options: Oranges have Vitamin C which acts as an antioxidant protecting lung tissue
Oranges are yellow and make you happy
Oranges have iron to boost energy
Oranges contain fat which coats the lungs
steps.explanation: Vitamin C in oranges is an antioxidant that protects lung cells from damage. It also helps the body fight infections and reduces inflammation in the airways.

steps.question: How can you keep your lungs healthy and prevent breathing problems?
options: Eat Vitamin C rich fruits, exercise regularly, and avoid smoke
Stay in bed and avoid all physical activity
Eat more oily and fatty snacks
Breathe through your mouth only
steps.explanation: A combination of Vitamin C rich foods (like citrus fruits, amla, guava), regular exercise, and clean air keeps the lungs strong and healthy.

steps.question: What happens in the lungs when we breathe in?
options: Oxygen enters the blood and carbon dioxide leaves the body
Food gets digested and turned into energy
Blood cells are made from the air
Calcium is absorbed to strengthen bones
steps.explanation: The lungs are responsible for gas exchange. When we breathe in, oxygen passes into the blood through tiny air sacs called alveoli. When we breathe out, carbon dioxide (a waste gas) leaves the body.

name: Stomach
description: Without enough dietary fiber, digestion slows down, causing stomach pain, bloating, and discomfort.

steps.question: Rohan's stomach hurts after meals and he feels bloated. What is missing from his diet?
options: Dietary Fiber
Vitamin A
Protein
Fat
steps.explanation: Dietary fiber helps food move through the digestive system smoothly. Without enough fiber, digestion becomes slow and the stomach feels uncomfortable.

steps.question: Which food will give Rohan the fiber he needs for good digestion?
steps.explanation: Oats are an excellent source of soluble fiber. Fiber helps food move through the intestines and keeps your digestion working well.

steps.question: Why do oats help with indigestion and stomach problems?
options: Oats have lots of fiber that helps food move through the digestive tract
Oats are soft so they are easy to eat
Oats have Vitamin C which kills stomach bacteria
Oats have sugar which gives fast energy
steps.explanation: Oats contain both soluble and insoluble fiber. Soluble fiber forms a gel-like substance that slows digestion in a good way, while insoluble fiber adds bulk to help food move through the intestines.

steps.question: What is the best way to prevent indigestion and keep your stomach healthy?
options: Eat lots of fruits, vegetables, and whole grains every day and drink plenty of water
Eat fast food and fried snacks every meal
Skip meals to give your stomach a rest
Drink more soda to help with digestion
steps.explanation: Eating fiber-rich foods like fruits, vegetables, legumes, and whole grains, along with drinking plenty of water, keeps the digestive system healthy and prevents indigestion.

steps.question: Where does most chemical digestion of food happen in the human body?
options: In the small intestine
In the mouth
In the large intestine
In the liver
steps.explanation: Most chemical digestion and nutrient absorption happens in the small intestine. Digestive enzymes from the pancreas and bile from the liver break down food into small molecules that are absorbed into the blood.

name: Brain
description: The brain needs Omega-3 fatty acids to build nerve connections and focus properly.
symptoms: finds it hard to focus or study

steps.question: Rohan cannot focus in school and forgets things easily. Which nutrient does his brain need?
options: Omega-3 Fatty Acids
Iron
Vitamin D
Calcium
steps.explanation: Omega-3 fatty acids are essential for brain health. They help build the membranes around brain cells and support communication between neurons, which improves focus and memory.

steps.question: Which food is the best source of Omega-3 to help Rohan's brain?
steps.explanation: Walnuts are one of the best plant sources of Omega-3 fatty acids. They even look like a tiny brain, which is a good reminder to eat them for brain health!

steps.question: Why do walnuts help improve concentration and memory?
options: They contain Omega-3 fatty acids that build and repair brain cells
They are crunchy so they wake you up
They have Vitamin C for energy
They contain iron for healthy blood
steps.explanation: Walnuts are rich in ALA, a type of Omega-3 fatty acid. Omega-3s are a key building block of the brain and help nerve cells communicate better, improving focus and memory.

steps.question: How can you keep your brain sharp and improve concentration?
options: Eat Omega-3 rich foods, sleep well, exercise, and read regularly
Play video games all night instead of sleeping
Eat lots of sugary snacks for quick brain energy
Avoid all physical activity to save brain energy
steps.explanation: Eating Omega-3 rich foods like walnuts, flaxseeds, and fish, combined with good sleep, exercise, and mental activities like reading, keeps the brain healthy and focused.

steps.question: What is the name of the long thread-like part of a nerve cell that carries messages?
options: Axon
Nucleus
Dendrite
Cell membrane
steps.explanation: The axon is the long, thread-like part of a neuron (nerve cell) that transmits electrical signals away from the cell body to other neurons or muscles. Omega-3 fatty acids help maintain the myelin sheath that covers and protects axons.

name: Skin
description: Without enough Vitamin E, skin becomes dry, itchy, and inflamed.
symptoms: small rashes appear on arms or face

steps.question: Rohan's skin is dry, itchy, and has red patches. Which vitamin is he missing?
options: Vitamin E
Vitamin A
Vitamin B12
Vitamin K
steps.explanation: Vitamin E is an antioxidant that protects skin cells from damage. Without it, skin becomes dry, inflamed, and prone to irritation.

steps.question: Which food will give Rohan Vitamin E to heal his skin?
steps.explanation: Almonds are one of the richest sources of Vitamin E. Just a small handful provides a good amount of this skin-protecting vitamin.

steps.question: How do almonds help someone with dry, itchy skin?
options: Almonds are rich in Vitamin E which protects and moisturizes skin cells
Almonds are hard and scratching with them relieves the itch
Almonds contain calcium which makes skin firm
Almonds have iron which makes skin look red and healthy
steps.explanation: Vitamin E in almonds is a powerful antioxidant that protects skin cell membranes from damage. It also helps the skin retain moisture and reduces inflammation.

steps.question: How can you keep your skin healthy and prevent dermatitis?
options: Eat Vitamin E rich foods, drink plenty of water, and protect skin from harsh sun
Apply cooking oil directly to the skin every day
Eat lots of fried and junk food for oily skin
Avoid bathing to keep skin's natural oils
steps.explanation: Eating foods rich in Vitamin E (almonds, sunflower seeds, spinach), staying hydrated, and protecting skin from sun damage all help maintain healthy, smooth skin.

steps.question: What is the outermost layer of the skin called?
options: Epidermis
Dermis
Hypodermis
Cortex
steps.explanation: The epidermis is the outermost layer of the skin. It acts as a protective barrier against the environment and is the layer most affected by Vitamin E deficiency and dermatitis.

name: Heart
description: Low potassium can cause irregular heartbeat and weaken the heart muscles over time.

steps.question: Rohan's heart sometimes beats irregularly and he tires quickly. Which mineral is he missing?
options: Potassium
Calcium
Sodium
Magnesium
steps.explanation: Potassium is essential for healthy heart muscle function. It helps regulate the heartbeat and keeps blood pressure at a healthy level.

steps.question: Which food is the best source of potassium for a healthy heart?
steps.explanation: Bananas are famous for being high in potassium. They help keep the heart beating regularly and blood pressure in check.

steps.question: Why do bananas help keep the heart healthy?
options: They are rich in potassium which regulates heartbeat and blood pressure
They are sweet which gives the heart energy to pump
They contain Vitamin D which strengthens heart muscle
They have fiber which cleans the blood
steps.explanation: Potassium in bananas helps control the electrical signals that make the heart beat. It also balances sodium levels, which helps keep blood pressure normal and reduces strain on the heart.

steps.question: How can you keep your heart healthy and prevent cardiovascular problems?
options: Eat potassium-rich fruits and vegetables, exercise regularly, and avoid too much salt
Eat salty snacks and fried food every day
Avoid all exercise to protect the heart
Drink lots of energy drinks for a strong heartbeat
steps.explanation: Regular exercise strengthens the heart muscle. Eating potassium-rich foods (bananas, sweet potato, tomatoes) and reducing salt intake helps maintain healthy blood pressure and protects the heart.

steps.question: How many chambers does the human heart have?
options: 4 chambers (2 atria and 2 ventricles)
2 chambers (1 atrium and 1 ventricle)
3 chambers (1 atrium and 2 ventricles)
6 chambers (3 atria and 3 ventricles)
steps.explanation: The human heart has 4 chambers: the right atrium and right ventricle (which pump blood to the lungs) and the left atrium and left ventricle (which pump blood to the rest of the body).

name: Small Intestine
description: Without enough zinc, the small intestine cannot properly absorb nutrients from food.
symptoms: feels weak even after eating well

steps.question: Rohan eats well but still feels weak and gets sick often. Which mineral is his small intestine missing?
options: Zinc
Iron
Calcium
Sodium
steps.explanation: Zinc is needed for the small intestine to absorb nutrients properly. It also helps the immune system and speeds up healing of wounds.

steps.question: Which food will give Rohan the zinc he needs?
steps.explanation: Pumpkin seeds are an excellent plant-based source of zinc. They are easy to eat as a snack and provide a good amount of this important mineral.

steps.question: Why do pumpkin seeds help with malabsorption syndrome?
options: They contain zinc which helps the intestine absorb nutrients and supports immunity
They are crunchy and help clean the intestines like a brush
They have calcium which strengthens the intestine walls
They have Vitamin A which improves eyesight
steps.explanation: Zinc in pumpkin seeds is essential for healthy gut function. It helps the cells in the small intestine work properly so that nutrients from food can be absorbed into the body.

steps.question: How can you prevent malabsorption and keep your small intestine healthy?
options: Eat a balanced diet with zinc-rich foods, avoid junk food, and drink clean water
Eat only one big meal a day
Drink lots of soft drinks with meals
Avoid legumes and nuts completely
steps.explanation: A balanced diet including zinc-rich foods (seeds, legumes, nuts), clean drinking water, and avoiding processed junk food helps the small intestine stay healthy and absorb nutrients properly.

steps.question: What are the tiny finger-like projections in the small intestine that help absorb nutrients?
options: Villi
Cilia
Nephrons
Alveoli
steps.explanation: Villi are tiny finger-like projections that cover the inner wall of the small intestine. They increase the surface area of the intestine, helping it absorb as many nutrients as possible from digested food.

name: Large Intestine
description: Without enough dietary fiber, the large intestine moves waste too slowly, causing constipation.
symptoms: cannot go to the bathroom easily

steps.question: Rohan's stomach is hard and he has trouble going to the bathroom. What is missing from his diet?
options: Dietary Fiber
Vitamin C
Protein
Fat
steps.explanation: Dietary fiber adds bulk to waste and helps the large intestine move it along. Without fiber, waste moves too slowly and becomes hard, causing constipation.

steps.question: Which food will give Rohan the fiber he needs to prevent constipation?
steps.explanation: Whole wheat bread is rich in insoluble fiber which adds bulk to stools and helps them move through the large intestine more easily.

steps.question: Why does whole wheat bread help prevent constipation?
options: It has insoluble fiber that adds bulk to waste and speeds up movement through the intestine
It is soft and easy to digest so it does not stress the intestine
It has Vitamin D which strengthens the intestine walls
It has iron which cleans the intestine
steps.explanation: Whole wheat bread contains insoluble fiber, which does not dissolve in water. Instead, it adds bulk and roughage to the waste in your large intestine, helping it move through more quickly and easily.

steps.question: What is the best way to prevent constipation?
options: Eat plenty of fiber-rich foods, drink 8 glasses of water daily, and exercise regularly
Eat lots of cheese and dairy products every day
Drink less water so the intestines do not have too much liquid
Avoid fruits and vegetables and eat only cooked rice
steps.explanation: Fiber-rich foods (whole grains, fruits, vegetables, legumes), drinking enough water, and physical activity all work together to keep the large intestine healthy and prevent constipation.

steps.question: What is the main job of the large intestine?
options: Absorbs water from undigested food and forms solid waste
Digests proteins from the food we eat
Produces digestive enzymes to break down food
Absorbs vitamins and minerals from digested food
steps.explanation: The main job of the large intestine is to absorb water and salts from the material that was not digested in the small intestine. It then forms this remaining material into solid waste (feces) which is stored until it leaves the body.
